warning: `/home/jfeng/.cargo/config` is deprecated in favor of `config.toml`

  = help: if you need to support cargo 1.38 or earlier, you can symlink `config` to `config.toml`
    Checking c4-rust v0.1.0 (/data1/jfeng/tcode_win/output/c4/c4-rust)
error[E0502]: cannot borrow `*self` as mutable because it is also borrowed as immutable
   --> src/c4.rs:252:31
    |
250 |                     let ident = &self.source[start..self.p];
    |                                  ----------- immutable borrow occurs here
251 |                     let hash = Self::hash_ident(ident);
252 |                     let idx = self.lookup_or_insert_id(ident, hash);
    |                               ^^^^^-------------------^^^^^^^^^^^^^
    |                               |    |
    |                               |    immutable borrow later used by call
    |                               mutable borrow occurs here

error[E0502]: cannot borrow `*self` as mutable because it is also borrowed as immutable
   --> src/c4.rs:504:21
    |
502 |                 let sym = &self.symbols[d];
    |                            ------------ immutable borrow occurs here
503 |                 if sym.class == CLASS_SYS {
504 |                     self.push_op(sym.val);
    |                     ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ mutable borrow occurs here
...
515 |                 self.ty = sym.ty;
    |                           ------ immutable borrow later used here

error[E0502]: cannot borrow `*self` as mutable because it is also borrowed as immutable
   --> src/c4.rs:506:21
    |
502 |                 let sym = &self.symbols[d];
    |                            ------------ immutable borrow occurs here
...
506 |                     self.push_op(OP_JSR);
    |                     ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ mutable borrow occurs here
507 |                     self.push_op(sym.val);
    |                                  ------- immutable borrow later used here

error[E0502]: cannot borrow `*self` as mutable because it is also borrowed as immutable

diagnosis_plan.summary: The active E0384 likely comes from a local mutability mismatch around `t` in `src/c4.rs`; read the surrounding Rust function and corresponding C/spec evidence before planning a minimal semantic-preserving local fix.
diagnosis_plan.edit_strategy: Inspect the Rust `expr` implementation around the failing loop, identify where `t` is declared and how it maps to the original C parser state variable, then apply a narrow local edit in `src/c4.rs` to align Rust mutability/ownership with the original semantics rather than introducing any stub or broader refactor.
reasoning: The reported error is isolated to one assignment site, but the correct repair depends on whether `t` is intended as a loop-carried mutable temporary or a shadowed per-branch value.
Reading the surrounding Rust range is necessary to see the declaration of `t`, the containing control flow, and whether additional immutable reassignments exist nearby.

Reading the corresponding region in `c/c4.c` helps confirm the intended parser-expression logic and prevents making a Rust-only mutability change that accidentally diverges from the C source behavior.
Spec files are worth checking because this is a language-port core module, and semantic preservation of expression parsing and type tracking matters more than merely silencing the compiler.

Keyword search for `let t` and `t = self.ty` will quickly reveal all declarations and reassignments of `t` in `src/c4.rs`, which is important for choosing between making a binding mutable versus restructuring shadowing.

Searching for `expr(` across sources helps locate the corresponding implementation region if line mapping is imperfect and can reveal additional translation patterns already used elsewhere in the file.

updated_summary: Updated `src/c4.rs` to declare `t` as mutable in `expr`, addressing the active E0384 at line 635; recompile is needed to see the next batch.
structured_summary: Make the local temporary `t` mutable in `src/c4.rs` so repeated assignments inside `expr` match the original C semantics and fix E0384.

current_summary: Updated `src/c4.rs` to declare `t` as mutable in `expr`, addressing the active E0384 at line 635; recompile is needed to see the next batch.

diagnosis_plan.summary: Active batch is confined to borrow-checker conflicts in src/c4.rs, likely fixable by shortening immutable borrows and copying needed symbol/source data into locals before mutable self calls.
diagnosis_plan.edit_strategy: Read the exact Rust spans around the failing sites plus the Symbol/lookup_or_insert_id definitions, then make small in-place edits in src/c4.rs: for tokenizing identifiers, avoid holding a slice of self.source across a mutable self call by converting the needed identifier bytes into an owned local or otherwise ending the borrow before lookup_or_insert_id; for expression handling, replace long-lived `let sym = &self.symbols[d]` borrows with copied scalar locals (`class`, `val`, `ty`) or narrower indexing so all immutable borrows end before `self.push_op(...)` and later assignments to `self.ty`.
reasoning: All current diagnostics are E0502 in one Rust file, so this round should focus tightly on src/c4.rs rather than broader project exploration.
The first error depends on the signature and behavior of lookup_or_insert_id, so that definition must be located before choosing whether to clone identifier data or restructure control flow.
The remaining errors arise from borrowing a symbol entry immutably while mutating self via push_op; reading Symbol confirms whether class/val/ty are Copy and can be extracted into locals safely.
Reading c4.c around lexer/parser logic provides translation intent for the affected sections, ensuring borrow-fix refactors preserve original behavior rather than introducing ad hoc changes.
No missing module/file issue is present in this batch, so no file creation planning is needed; targeted local borrow-scope reductions are the expected repair action.

handoff_summary_for_next_iteration:
This round fixed the reported E0502 borrow conflicts in src/c4.rs by shortening immutable borrow lifetimes around identifier lookup in next() and symbol access in expr(). The end-of-round build no longer shows those borrow-checker errors, so these changes were effective and the baseline advanced.

What changed effectively: the targeted borrow-scope restructuring resolved the active compile blockers from the previous baseline. What was not addressed: the crate still emits warnings, notably private_interfaces involving C4Error versus pub methods.

Next round should avoid revisiting the old E0502 fixes unless a regression appears; those errors are no longer the active issue. Prioritize running the build to completion from this accepted baseline, capturing the next real blocker after these fixes, and then address remaining compile errors or warnings in order, with special attention to visibility/API consistency around C4Error if warnings are being cleaned up.
